response.text:
# REFLECTION

r0c0 is in acute survival crisis within a cascading islanded microgrid collapse. At 11.5% SoC (~13 minutes of load continuity), the household is below its own minimum sharing threshold (15%) and has exhausted all peer-to-peer supply channels—every peer has rejected outbound requests citing their own critical depletion or cluster-wide inbound-only protocols. The repeated rejection pattern (r1c1, r2c2, r4c4 all refusing) confirms that horizontal peer sharing is no longer viable; the entire islanded cluster is in synchronized freefall. r0c0 is marked as distrusted by r2c2, further isolating it from lateral support. The only viable lifeline is immediate escalation to the aggregator (dr_aggregator/agg_gridflex) for emergency supply injection or grid restoration. Continuing to request from peers wastes remaining autonomy; all policy must pivot to aggregator-only coordination with maximum urgency and zero outbound sharing to preserve the critical 13-minute survival window.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.95
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority:
  - circle: dr_aggregator
    weight: 1.0
distrusted_peers:
  - r2c2
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded cluster total collapse; all peers in critical depletion and inbound-only mode. r0c0 at 11.5% SoC (13 min autonomy). Peer-to-peer sharing exhausted. Aggregator emergency supply injection mandatory for survival. Zero outbound enforced."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
